steps.title: Set up a time equation
steps.content: The trip lasted $4$ hours total. Since $x$ is the time on the highway and $y$ is the time on local roads, the times add to the total: $x+y=4$.

steps.title: Set up a distance equation
steps.content: Distance equals rate times time. On the highway, the bus covered $55x$ miles; on local roads, it covered $25y$ miles. Together these equal the $160$ mile trip: $55x+25y=160$.

steps.title: Match the correct system
steps.content:
The system is $55x+25y=160$ and $x+y=4$.
  • A) $55x+25y=4$, $x+y=160$ swaps the totals: the distances equal $4$ and times equal $160$, which is impossible.
  • B) $55x+25y=160$, $x+y=4$ matches the distance and time equations derived above.
  • C) $25x+55y=4$, $x+y=160$ both swaps totals and assigns $25$ mph to the highway time $x$ instead of $55$ mph.
  • D) $25x+55y=160$, $x+y=4$ has the right totals but pairs the $25$ mph local rate with the highway time $x$ and the $55$ mph highway rate with the local time $y$. That confirms B is correct.
